text: This week we're projected to get some of the coldest temperatures that South Florida has seen in the past 15 years and this tree right behind me is not going to enjoy that. As you can see it's already lost a whole bunch of its leaves from the earlier cold snaps and if it actually gets down to 32 it could possibly kill this tree. But I don't give up so easily so it's time to get creative. If you saw my previous video you know that I already built some wind and sun protection to try to help this tree establish at its new home but that's not going to be enough to keep the cold out. So what I've started doing right here is I built this PVC frame over the top of the tree and we're going to cover the entire tree to try to preserve the heat in the inside. Underneath each one of these PVC pipes is a piece of rebar that keeps it anchored into the ground and then a cross section of PVC pipe to keep it connected to each one of the other sides. Next thing that I did was cover each one of the sides of the frame with plastic wrap. This will create a greenhouse-like effect and keep the wind out as well as the heat on the inside. The problem with the plastic though is it doesn't allow for ventilation and if it touches any of the branches it'll make them even colder because it holds onto that cold. So over the top of our frame I'm going to be adding this frost cloth that'll not only help keep the heat in and the cold out but it also allows for ventilation and it's safe for any of the branches that might bump into it. Think of this like putting on a jacket to help keep the cold out and the warm on the inside except for the fact that trees don't produce their own heat. So in order to keep heat on the inside you have to add some. So to add some warmth here on the inside I've added incandescent string lights to the base of the trunk which will help to add heat to it and the roots and as the heat builds it'll rise up to the rest of the canopy. Now that it's fully covered this is what it looks like. It doesn't look that pretty but I hope that it will be effective. When I look on in the inside here I can already feel that it's much warmer here. During the days I can raise this side so that there's nice ventilation for the tree and then at night when it gets cold I can shut it back down. Now I'm just going to deeply water the tree and we'll just have to see how this next week goes. It's already starting to get warm in here so I'm pretty hopeful that this will work but we just have to wait and see. Until next time get outside and see what you can find.
